title: Index signature with a number key
description: `{[k: number]: T}` — TS lets you declare number-keyed index signatures. JS object keys are always strings at runtime, so the resolver normalises this to the same shape as `{[k: string]: T}` and the validator behaves identically.
notes: TS DIVERGENCE: At runtime, all object keys are strings; the number key type constraint is enforced only by the TS compiler. The validator accepts any own enumerable key whose value satisfies T.
